tasks.title: Project Scaffold and Cargo Configuration
tasks.description: Initialize Rust project structure with specified directories and dependencies per PRD crate stack
tasks.details: Run `cargo new forgeStat --bin`; create directories: core/, tui/, gui/; add Cargo.toml dependencies: ratatui, egui_ratatui, eframe, octocrab, tokio={version=1, features=[full]}, serde, serde_json, toml, chrono, clap={features=[derive]}; implement basic main.rs with clap for --tui/--gui flags parsing repo argument (owner/repo format).
tasks.testStrategy: cargo check; cargo test --lib; verify clap parses `forgeStat owner/repo`, `forgeStat owner/repo --gui`, `forgeStat owner/repo --tui`; directory structure matches forgeStat/core/tui/gui/main.rs
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: 1) Project initialization and directory structure setup, 2) Cargo.toml dependency configuration and basic CLI parsing in main.rs

tasks.title: Data Models Implementation
tasks.description: Define complete RepoSnapshot struct and all sub-structs per PRD data model specification
tasks.details: In core/models.rs: impl RepoSnapshot {pub fetched_at: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>, repo: RepoMeta, stars: StarHistory, issues: IssueStats, pull_requests: PrStats, contributors: ContributorStats, releases: Vec<Release>, velocity: VelocityStats, security_alerts: Option<SecurityAlerts>}; derive Serialize/Deserialize for all; define all nested structs with relevant fields (e.g. StarHistory with total_count, sparkline_30d/90d/365d vectors; IssueStats with total_open, by_label: HashMap<String,Vec<Issue>>, etc.).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: serde roundtrip serialization/deserialization of sample RepoSnapshot JSON; validate all fields present and types correct
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Main RepoSnapshot and core structs, 2) StarHistory and IssueStats with nested collections, 3) Remaining metrics structs (PRs, contributors, etc.) with Serialize derives and tests

tasks.title: Config Management and GitHub PAT Handling
tasks.description: Implement GITHUB_TOKEN env var reading and ~/.config/forgeStat/config.toml storage
tasks.details: In core/config.rs: use std::env; use toml; use dirs::config_dir(); fn load_token() -> Result<String> {if let Ok(token) = env::var("GITHUB_TOKEN") {return Ok(token);} let config_path = config_dir().unwrap().join("forgeStat").join("config.toml"); if config_path.exists() {let config: toml::Value = std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path)?.parse()?; return Ok(config["github_token"].as_str().unwrap().to_string());} Err("No token".into())} fn save_token(token: &str) -> Result<()> {let config_path = ...; std::fs::create_dir_all(config_path.parent().unwrap())?; std::fs::write(config_path, format!("github_token = \"\"", token))?; Ok(())}
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: mock env var returns token; mock config file read/write; verify token loading precedence (env > file); test missing token error handling
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Token loading logic (env var precedence over config file), 2) Config file read/write with directory creation and TOML parsing

tasks.title: GitHub API Client with Octocrab Integration
tasks.description: Create core GitHub client using octocrab with token auth supporting public/private repos
tasks.details: In core/github_client.rs: use octocrab::Octocrab; pub struct GitHubClient {octocrab: Octocrab} impl GitHubClient {pub async fn new(token: Option<&str>) -> Self {let builder = Octocrab::builder(); if let Some(t) = token {builder.personal_token(t);} Self{octocrab: builder.build().unwrap()}}} implement methods: repos(owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Result<RepoMeta>, stargazers() -> Result<StarHistory>, issues() -> Result<IssueStats>, etc.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests with GitHub mock server or public repo (e.g. rust-lang/rust); verify authenticated vs unauthenticated rate limits; test private repo access requires token
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down by API method stubs: 1) Client initialization and auth, 2) repos() metadata fetch, 3) Basic metrics stubs (stargazers/issues), 4) Complex metrics stubs (contributors/releases)

tasks.title: Local Cache System Implementation
tasks.description: Implement cache.json storage in ~/.local/share/forgeStat/<owner>/<repo> with TTL and sync status
tasks.details: In core/cache.rs: use dirs::data_local_dir(); pub struct Cache {path: PathBuf} impl Cache {pub fn new(owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Self {let base = data_local_dir().unwrap().join("forgeStat").join(owner).join(repo); std::fs::create_dir_all(&base).unwrap(); Self{path: base.join("cache.json")}} pub async fn load(&self) -> Result<Option<(RepoSnapshot, chrono::DateTime<Utc>)>> {...} pub async fn save(&self, snapshot: &RepoSnapshot) -> Result<()> {...} pub fn is_stale(&self, ttl_mins: u64) -> bool {...}}
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: cache read/write roundtrip; TTL calculation (>15min = stale); offline mode loads last cache; verify cache path structure
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Cache path management and directory creation, 2) JSON read/write with TTL logic and serde integration

tasks.title: Star History API and Sparkline Implementation
tasks.description: Fetch stargazer timestamps via GitHub Stargazers API with Accept: application/vnd.github.star+json
tasks.details: In core/metrics/stars.rs: use GitHubClient; impl GitHubClient {pub async fn star_history(&self, owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Result<StarHistory> {let stargazers = self.octocrab.repos(owner, repo).stargazers().send().await?; let timestamps: Vec<DateTime<Utc>> = stargazers.items.iter().map(|star| star.starred_at).collect(); let sparkline_30d = generate_sparkline(&timestamps, chrono::Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::days(30)); ...}} fn generate_sparkline(timestamps: &[DateTime<Utc>], period_start: DateTime<Utc>) -> Vec<i32> { /* bin timestamps into days, count stars per bin */ }
tasks.testStrategy: Test with public repo (e.g. ratatui-org/ratatui); verify sparkline_30d/90d/365d vectors have correct length and values; mock API response tests
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Stargazers API call with custom Accept header, 2) Timestamp collection and filtering, 3) Sparkline generation algorithm for multiple periods (30d/90d/365d)

tasks.title: Issues Breakdown with Label Grouping and Age Sorting
tasks.description: Fetch open issues grouped by label, sorted by age, including unlabelled
tasks.details: In core/metrics/issues.rs: pub async fn issues_stats(&self, owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Result<IssueStats> {let issues = self.octocrab.issues(owner, repo).list().state(reqwest::StatusCode::OK).send().await?; let mut by_label: HashMap<String, Vec<Issue>> = HashMap::new(); let mut unlabelled = vec![]; for issue in issues {let labels: Vec<String> = issue.labels.iter().map(|l| l.name.clone()).collect(); if labels.is_empty() {unlabelled.push(issue);} else {for label in labels {by_label.entry(label).or_insert(vec![]).push(issue.clone());}}} ... sort by created_at ascending (oldest first)}
tasks.testStrategy: Verify label grouping correct; oldest issues first; unlabelled bucket works; total_open count matches
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Issues API fetch and label extraction, 2) Grouping logic (by_label HashMap + unlabelled) with age sorting

tasks.title: PR Stats Implementation (Open, Merged, Draft, Merge Time)
tasks.description: Fetch PR stats including 30-day merged count, avg merge time, draft vs ready
tasks.details: In core/metrics/prs.rs: async fn pr_stats(&self, owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Result<PrStats> {let open_prs = self.octocrab.pulls(owner, repo).list().state(reqwest::StatusCode::OK).send().await?; let mut merged_30d = 0; let mut total_merge_times = vec![]; for pr in self.octocrab.pulls(owner, repo).list().state("all").send().await? {if pr.merged_at.is_some() && (Utc::now() - pr.merged_at.unwrap()) < Duration::days(30) {merged_30d += 1;} if let (Some(created), Some(merged)) = (pr.created_at, pr.merged_at) {total_merge_times.push((merged - created).num_days());}} PrStats{open_count: open_prs.len(), merged_30d, avg_merge_days: total_merge_times.iter().sum::<i64>() as f64 / total_merge_times.len() as f64, draft_count: open_prs.iter().filter(|pr| pr.draft).count(), ...}
tasks.testStrategy: Verify counts accurate against public repo; avg merge time calculation; draft filtering
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Open PRs count and draft filtering, 2) Merged PRs 30-day count, 3) Average merge time calculation across all history

tasks.title: Contributor Activity Metrics
tasks.description: Top 10 commit contributors, new contributors last 30 days, total unique
tasks.details: In core/metrics/contributors.rs: async fn contributor_stats(&self, owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Result<ContributorStats> {let commits = self.octocrab.repos(owner, repo).commits().list().send().await?; let mut contrib_counts: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new(); let thirty_days_ago = Utc::now() - Duration::days(30); let mut new_contribs = HashSet::new(); for commit in commits {let author = commit.author.login; *contrib_counts.entry(author.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1; if commit.author.date > thirty_days_ago && !new_contribs.contains(&author) {new_contribs.insert(author);}} let top10: Vec<_> = contrib_counts.iter().sorted_by(|a,b| b.1.cmp(a.1)).take(10).collect(); ...}
tasks.testStrategy: Test commit aggregation; top10 sorting descending; new contrib detection
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Commit aggregation and top-10 sorting, 2) New contributors detection (last 30 days) with HashSet deduplication

tasks.title: Release Cadence Metrics
tasks.description: Last 5 releases with days since last, average interval
tasks.details: In core/metrics/releases.rs: async fn release_stats(&self, owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Result<Vec<Release>> {let releases = self.octocrab.repos(owner, repo).releases().list().per_page(5u8).send().await?; let mut releases_vec: Vec<Release> = releases.items.into_iter().map(|r| Release{name: r.name, tag: r.tag_name, published_at: r.published_at, days_since: (Utc::now() - r.published_at.unwrap()).num_days()}).collect(); if releases_vec.len() > 1 {let intervals: Vec<i64> = releases_vec.windows(2).map(|w| (w[0].published_at - w[1].published_at).num_days()).collect(); releases_vec[0].avg_interval = intervals.iter().sum::<i64>() as f64 / intervals.len() as f64;} releases_vec}
tasks.testStrategy: Verify last 5 releases fetched; days_since calculation; avg_interval accurate
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Fetch last 5 releases mapping, 2) Days_since and average interval calculations with windows iteration

tasks.details: In core/metrics/velocity.rs: async fn velocity_stats(&self, owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Result<VelocityStats> {let eight_weeks_ago = Utc::now() - Duration::weeks(8); let mut weekly_data = vec![Default::default(); 8]; // Fetch issues/PRs with created/closed dates, bin into weeks, count opened/closed per week}
tasks.testStrategy: Verify 8 weeks of data; opened vs closed counts per week; date binning correct
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Issues velocity (opened/closed per week), 2) PRs velocity (opened/closed per week), 3) Date binning into 8-week buckets

tasks.title: Security Alerts (Dependabot) with Auth Check
tasks.description: Fetch Dependabot alerts count (requires security_events scope)
tasks.details: In core/metrics/security.rs: async fn security_alerts(&self, owner: &str, repo: &str) -> Result<Option<SecurityAlerts>> {match self.octocrab.repos(owner, repo).security_advisories().list().send().await {Ok(alerts) => Some(SecurityAlerts{open_count: alerts.len(), ...}), Err(e) if e.to_string().contains("scope") => None, Err(e) => Err(e),}}
tasks.testStrategy: Test with authenticated PAT having security_events scope; verify None returned without scope; count accurate

tasks.title: RepoSnapshot Fetcher Orchestration
tasks.description: Main function to fetch complete RepoSnapshot coordinating all metrics
tasks.details: In core/snapshot.rs: pub async fn fetch_snapshot(client: &GitHubClient, cache: &Cache, owner: &str, repo: &str, force_refresh: bool) -> Result<RepoSnapshot> {if !force_refresh && let Some((snapshot, fetched_at)) = cache.load().await? && !cache.is_stale(15) {return Ok(snapshot);} let repo_meta = client.repos(owner, repo).await?; let (stars, issues, prs, ...) = tokio::join!(client.star_history(owner,repo), client.issues_stats(owner,repo), ...); Ok(RepoSnapshot{fetched_at: Utc::now(), repo: repo_meta, stars, issues, prs, contributors, releases, velocity, security_alerts,})}
tasks.testStrategy: End-to-end test: fetch complete snapshot for public repo; verify cache hit/miss behavior; force_refresh bypasses cache
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: 1) Cache check and early return logic, 2) Parallel tokio::join! for all 9 metrics, 3) RepoSnapshot assembly and save

tasks.title: Ratatui Core Layout and Metric Panels
tasks.description: Implement TUI with 7 metric panels, tab navigation, status bar with sync/offline indicator
tasks.details: In tui/app.rs: use ratatui::{prelude::*, widgets::*}; struct App {snapshot: Option<RepoSnapshot>, cache: Cache, selected_panel: usize, ...} impl App {fn render(&mut self, f: &mut Frame) {let chunks = Layout::default().constraints([Constraint::Percentage(25);4]).split(f.area()); // 7 panels in grid; render StarHistory sparkline with ratatui::widgets::Sparkline; Issues table with Table; PR stats cards; etc.; status bar: if offline {Paragraph::new("🔴 OFFLINE").red()} else if stale {"🟡 STALE"} else {"🟢 LIVE | Last sync: X mins ago"};}}
tasks.testStrategy: cargo run forgeStat ratatui-org/ratatui --tui; verify all 7 panels render data; tab/shift-tab cycles panels; r refreshes; q quits; ? shows help
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide by UI component: 1) Layout and 7-panel grid, 2) Star sparkline widget, 3) Issues table, 4) PR/contributor cards, 5) Status bar with sync indicators

tasks.title: TUI Event Handling and Navigation
tasks.description: Implement key bindings: Tab navigation, r refresh, q quit, ? help, arrow scrolling
tasks.details: In tui/app.rs: loop {match crossterm::event::read()? {Event::Key(key) => match key.code {KeyCode::Tab => self.selected_panel = (self.selected_panel + 1) % 7; KeyCode::Char('r') => {self.snapshot = Some(fetch_snapshot(&self.client, &self.cache, owner, repo, true).await?); self.cache.save(&self.snapshot.as_ref().unwrap()).await?;}, KeyCode::Char('q') => break; KeyCode::Char('?') => show_help_overlay(); KeyCode::Up|Down => scroll_active_panel(); _ => {}}}}}
tasks.testStrategy: Manual testing: tab cycles panels; r forces refresh and updates status; q exits cleanly; arrow keys scroll content
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Key binding matching (tab/r/q/?/arrows), 2) Async refresh integration with cache update and error display

tasks.title: Egui_ratatui Desktop GUI Wrapper
tasks.description: Implement --gui mode using eframe wrapping ratatui backend via egui_ratatui
tasks.details: In gui/main.rs: use eframe::egui; use egui_ratatui; fn main() {let options = eframe::NativeOptions::default(); eframe::run_native("Repowatch", options, Box::new(|cc| {let mut app = TuiApp::new(cc.egui_ctx.clone(), args.owner_repo()); // TuiApp wraps ratatui App; Box::new(app)}));} impl eframe::App for TuiApp {fn update(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context, frame: &mut eframe::Frame) {egui_ratatui::EguiRatatui::new().show(ctx, |f| {self.ratatui_app.render(f);}); if ctx.input(|i| i.key_pressed(egui::Key::R)) {self.refresh();}}}
tasks.testStrategy: cargo run forgeStat ratatui-org/ratatui --gui; verify desktop window opens with identical TUI content; refresh button/keyboard works
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) eframe setup and native window, 2) egui_ratatui integration wrapping TUI App, 3) GUI event handling (refresh/keyboard)

tasks.title: Main CLI Integration and Launch Modes
tasks.description: Integrate TUI/GUI launch from main.rs based on CLI flags with repo parsing
tasks.details: In main.rs: #[tokio::main] async fn main() {let matches = clap::Command::new("forgeStat").arg(Arg::Positional("repo", ...)).arg(arg!(--gui)).arg(arg!(--tui)).get_matches(); let repo = matches.get_one::<String>("repo").unwrap(); let (owner, repo_name) = parse_repo(repo)?; // "owner/repo" -> ("owner", "repo"); if matches.get_flag("gui") {gui::run(owner, repo_name).await;} else {tui::run(owner, repo_name).await;}}
tasks.testStrategy: Test all modes: default TUI, explicit --tui, --gui; repo parsing edge cases; error handling for invalid repo format

Use uuid crate for snapshot_history_id. On startup, scan history/ and delete files older than 30 days using chrono::Utc::now(). Store scroll positions in state.json.

subtasks.title: Update RepoSnapshot Struct with New Fields

Add DateTime<Utc> for previous_snapshot_at and Uuid for snapshot_history_id. Ensure proper derive macros for serde serialization. Update any existing serialization/deserialization code.
<info added on 2026-02-27T17:55:00.639Z>
Successfully implemented new fields on RepoSnapshot struct:

1. Added `uuid` crate to Cargo.toml with v4 and serde features
2. Updated RepoSnapshot struct in `src/core/models.rs`:
   - Added `previous_snapshot_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>` - references the previous snapshot's timestamp
   - Added `snapshot_history_id: Uuid` - unique identifier for each snapshot
3. Updated snapshot creation in `src/core/snapshot.rs` to:
   - Load existing cache to get previous snapshot timestamp
   - Generate new UUID for each snapshot
4. Updated `src/core/github_client.rs` fetch_snapshot function with new fields
5. Updated test helper functions in both models.rs and cache.rs
6. Added comprehensive tests:
   - test_new_fields_serialization: verifies new fields serialize/deserialize correctly
   - test_snapshot_with_previous: tests snapshot with previous_snapshot_at set
   - test_uuid_uniqueness: verifies 100 UUIDs are all unique

All 61 tests pass. The implementation follows existing code patterns and conventions.
</info added on 2026-02-27T17:55:00.639Z>

subtasks.title: Implement 30-Day History Purge on Startup

subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: mock filesystem with files at various ages, verify correct deletion logic

subtasks.title: Implement Rolling History Cache and State Persistence
subtasks.description: Maintain max 20 snapshots in history/, implement rotation logic, store scroll positions in state.json.
subtasks.details:
Before adding new snapshot, if history/ has 20+ files, delete oldest. Save snapshots as JSON with UUID filenames in history/. Implement state.json serde for scroll positions with proper error handling.
<info added on 2026-02-27T18:08:14.440Z>
Successfully implemented rolling history cache with 20 snapshot limit:

1. Added save_to_history() method to Cache:
   - Saves snapshot as JSON with UUID filename (<uuid>.json)
   - Maintains max 20 snapshots (MAX_HISTORY_SNAPSHOTS constant)
   - Automatically deletes oldest files when limit exceeded
   - Uses file modification time for age sorting
   - Returns path to saved history file

2. Added helper methods:
   - get_history_files(): Lists history files sorted by age (oldest first)
   - history_count(): Returns current number of history snapshots

3. Integrated into snapshot fetch flow:
   - After saving to cache.json, also saves to history/
   - Then purges old files (>30 days)

4. Added tests:
   - test_save_to_history_creates_file: verifies file creation with UUID name
   - test_save_to_history_rotation: verifies 20 snapshot limit enforced

All 67 tests pass.
</info added on 2026-02-27T18:08:14.440Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: create 25 snapshots, verify only 20 remain with correct oldest purged; test state.json persistence

tasks.title: Refactor src/tui/app.rs into Logical Modules
tasks.description: Split the 4733-line src/tui/app.rs file into focused, single-responsibility modules while preserving all functionality and passing all 125 tests.
tasks.details:
Extract the following modules from src/tui/app.rs using pub(crate) visibility to maintain encapsulation:

1. **tui/panels.rs** (~500 lines): Panel renderer functions for rendering all panel types (stars, issues, velocity, contributors, releases). Move render_panel() and related helper functions.

2. **tui/diff.rs** (~400 lines): Diff mode overlay logic including snapshot comparison, change highlighting (green for increases, red for decreases), and split-screen rendering.

3. **tui/zoom.rs** (~650 lines): Zoom overlay implementation for expanded panel views with full detail rendering and navigation.

4. **tui/mini_map.rs** (~240 lines): Mini-map modal rendering showing 12+ aggregated metrics across all panels with condensed 2-3 line layout.

5. **tui/command_palette.rs** (~400 lines): CommandPalette struct, state management, command parsing, autocomplete logic, and history navigation.

6. **tui/fuzzy_finder.rs** (~120 lines): Fuzzy finder overlay for searching and filtering within panels.

7. **tui/help.rs** (~150 lines): Help overlay modal with organized shortcut documentation (Global/Panel-Specific/Mouse sections).

8. **tui/event_loop.rs** (~350 lines): run_event_loop() function and event handling orchestration.

9. **tui/mouse.rs** (~190 lines): Mouse event handling including border detection, drag tracking, and panel resizing logic.

10. **tui/utils.rs** (~100 lines): Utility functions for formatting, calculations, and common operations used across modules.

Keep App struct and core methods (new(), update(), render()) in app.rs. Use pub(crate) for module-internal functions to prevent accidental external dependencies. Update all internal imports to use the new module paths. Maintain all existing test organization—tests can remain in their original modules or be moved with their corresponding code. Ensure no circular dependencies between modules. Use relative imports (super::) where appropriate for sibling module access.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run `cargo test` to verify all 125 tests pass after each module extraction (extract one module at a time).
2. Verify compilation with `cargo build` produces no warnings about unused items or visibility issues.
3. Check that all public APIs from app.rs remain accessible with identical signatures.
4. Manually test core workflows: navigate panels, toggle overlays (zoom, diff, mini-map, command palette, help), use keyboard shortcuts, verify mouse interactions work.
5. Confirm no behavioral changes by comparing output before/after refactoring on sample data.
6. Validate that pub(crate) visibility prevents unintended external access using `cargo doc --open` to inspect public API surface.
7. Test module interdependencies by temporarily removing imports to catch missing dependencies.

tasks.title: Create HealthScore computation module
tasks.description: Implement core health score computation with 4 sub-scores (Activity, Community, Maintenance, Growth) each 0-25, totaling 0-100.
tasks.details: Create src/core/health.rs. Define pub struct HealthScore { total: u8, activity: u8, community: u8, maintenance: u8, growth: u8, grade: HealthGrade }. pub enum HealthGrade { Excellent, Good, Fair, NeedsAttention, Critical }. Implement compute_health_score(snapshot: &RepoSnapshot) -> HealthScore with transparent formulas: Activity (commit velocity, PR merge rate/speed, recency), Community (contributor diversity, new contributors, issue engagement), Maintenance (release cadence, security alerts, issue resolution), Growth (star trends, fork/watchers). Thresholds: 90-100 Excellent(green), 75-89 Good(cyan), etc.

Add rendering in status bar: format!("Health: {}/100 ({:?})", score.total, score.grade) with color based on grade. Update mini-map.rs prominent display. Update zoom.rs stars view. tasks.title: Implement Star Milestone Prediction

Calculate weighted avg daily rate (30d:70%, 90d:30%). Next milestone = smallest > current_stars from [100,500,1000,5000,...]. days = (milestone - current) / daily_rate. 'Growth stalled' if rate <= 0.

tasks.title: Prepare forgeStat for Release with Cross-Platform Packaging and Installation Support
tasks.description: Set up forgeStat for production release by creating comprehensive Cargo.toml package metadata, platform-specific installation scripts, binary distribution via cargo-dist, and automated CI/CD release pipelines with proper versioning and documentation.
tasks.details: Create a complete release infrastructure for forgeStat across Windows, Linux, and macOS. Start by enhancing Cargo.toml[3] with complete [package] metadata including name, version following semantic versioning, authors, edition (2021), description, license (e.g., MIT or Apache-2.0), repository URL, and homepage. Add [package.metadata] sections[3] for tool-specific configurations. Create platform-specific installation scripts: Windows PowerShell script (install.ps1) that downloads the binary, verifies checksums, adds to PATH, and creates Start Menu shortcuts; Linux shell script (install.sh) handling apt/yum/pacman package managers or direct binary installation with systemd integration if needed; macOS shell script (install.sh) using Homebrew or direct installation with code signing. Set up cargo-dist[4] configuration in Cargo.toml under [package.metadata.dist] to automate binary builds for x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aarch64-apple-darwin, and x86_64-apple-darwin targets. Configure GitHub Actions workflows (.github/workflows/release.yml) to trigger on version tags, build binaries via cargo-dist, generate checksums, create GitHub releases with artifacts, and publish to crates.io. Ensure the CLI command 'forgeStat' (or 'forgestat') is properly registered in Cargo.toml [[bin]] section with correct path. Create comprehensive README.md with installation instructions for each platform, quick-start examples, and troubleshooting. Implement semantic versioning strategy (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) with changelog (CHANGELOG.md) documenting each release. Add license file (LICENSE or LICENSE.md) matching declared license. Configure cargo metadata output[1][5] to validate package structure using 'cargo metadata' command. Test that post-installation, the CLI command executes correctly and displays help/version information on all three platforms.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify Cargo.toml structure using 'cargo metadata --format-version 1' to ensure valid JSON output with all required fields[1][5]. Test Windows installation: run install.ps1 in PowerShell, verify binary downloads, PATH is updated, and 'forgeStat --version' executes from any directory. Test Linux installation: run install.sh on Ubuntu/Fedora/Arch, verify binary placement in /usr/local/bin or ~/.cargo/bin, and command executes. Test macOS installation: run install.sh, verify Homebrew formula or direct installation, and command executes on both Intel and Apple Silicon. Validate cargo-dist configuration by running 'cargo dist plan' and 'cargo dist build' locally, confirming all target binaries are generated. Test GitHub Actions workflow by creating a test tag and verifying release artifacts are created and checksums are correct. Verify README installation instructions match actual script behavior. Test that uninstallation (if applicable) cleanly removes binaries and PATH entries. Cross-platform integration test: install on each platform, run 'forgeStat --help', 'forgeStat --version', and a basic command (e.g., 'forgeStat <repo>') to confirm full functionality. Validate crates.io metadata by running 'cargo publish --dry-run' and checking for warnings.

Use [dependencies] section with versions ^0.28 for ratatui, ^0.38 for octocrab, etc. Set up basic main.rs with clap for repo parsing.

Add nested structs with relevant fields like Vec<Issue>, Vec<Pr>, etc. Use chrono::Utc for timestamps.

Test connectivity with simple repo GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}. Handle 403/429 rate limits with exponential backoff using tokio::time::sleep.

Generate sparkline ascii data (simple char array like ' ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█'). Store in StarHistory { total: u64, trend_30d: Vec<char>, ... }.

tasks.title: Security Alerts Fetcher
tasks.description: Fetch Dependabot alerts count (requires auth).
tasks.details: core/security.rs: If authed && security_events scope, client.repos(owner, repo).vulnerability_alerts().send(). Count open. Else Option::None. Show warning badge if >0.
tasks.testStrategy: Conditional logic test: with/without auth returns Option.

tasks.title: Full RepoSnapshot Fetch and Sync Orchestrator
tasks.description: Async function to fetch all metrics, build RepoSnapshot, handle cache/online/offline.
tasks.details: core/sync.rs: async fn fetch_snapshot(client: Octocrab, owner: &str, repo: &str, use_cache: bool) -> Result<RepoSnapshot>. Parallel tokio::join! for all fetchers. Check connectivity, respect cache TTL 15min. Manual refresh flag.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: full fetch public repo, verify all fields populated.

tasks.title: Ratatui TUI Layout and Navigation
tasks.description: Implement core TUI with 7 metric panels, tab navigation, status bar.
tasks.details: tui/app.rs: ratatui::Terminal<Crossterm>, AppState { snapshot: RepoSnapshot, mode: Live/Cache/Stale/Offline, focused_panel: usize }. Layout: 7 Block widgets (stars/issues/prs/etc), status: "Last synced X ago 🟢LIVE". Keys: tab/shift-tab cycle, r=refresh, q=quit, ? =help.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo run -- repo, verify panels render, tab cycles focus.

tasks.title: CLI Entry Point and Main Loop
tasks.description: Parse args owner/repo, launch TUI with auto-refresh every 10min.
tasks.details: main.rs: clap::Parser { repo: String }, parse "owner/repo". tokio::main async { let config = load_config(), client = make_client(), loop { snapshot = fetch(), tui.run(snapshot), tokio::time::sleep(10min) if !quit }. Handle offline gracefully.
tasks.testStrategy: End-to-end: cargo run -- repowatch rust-lang/rust, verify TUI launches, r refreshes, offline shows cache.

tasks.title: TUI Polish: Sparklines, Charts, Scroll, Help Overlay
tasks.description: Add sparkline rendering, bar charts, panel scrolling, ? help screen.
tasks.details: Extend tui/widgets.rs: Sparkline widget using ratatui::widgets::Sparkline, BarChart for velocity. Scrollable Paragraph for issue/PR lists. Modal Block for help keys. Highlight oldest issues red.
tasks.testStrategy: Visual verification: run app, check charts render ascii correctly.

tasks.title: Error Handling and Rate Limit Resilience
tasks.description: Graceful degradation for API errors, offline mode, stale cache.
tasks.details: anyhow::Result everywhere. On 429: show "Rate limited, using cache". Network error: "🔴 OFFLINE". Partial fetch: use cache where available. Status badges: 🟢LIVE 🟡STALE 🔴OFFLINE.
tasks.testStrategy: Mock network failures, verify fallback to cache.
